title: First Steps in AI Model Training - a case study
author: Carraro, Francesco
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This document outlines the journey of the  HyperLab  project, initially aimed at developing a mobile/web application for data analysis related to the SLab laboratory data at INAF-IAPS. Over time, HyperLab evolved into a versatile tool for the research community, incorporating advanced data analysis techniques and machine learning algorithms. The project emphasizes the importance of leveraging pre-built AI models to accelerate development, focusing on practical implementation rather than theoretical study. It details the challenges faced, such as finding suitable pre-trained models, running them on local PCs, and generating training data. The document also explains the process of training a model to convert natural language queries into SQL queries, highlighting the use of transformer models and the benefits of containerization for setting up the development environment
